blind_prediction.basis: Conservative non-EOF events from the wire_mesh real-app diagnostic with 60s debounce, excluding same-timestamp EOF burst.

notes: Only the hidden total has been revealed.
Event-level truth review is still required to classify which predicted events are false positives or whether any true placements are missing.

diagnosis: The blind app/AI prediction overcounted the hidden human total by 14.
Read the 18 as diagnostic dead-track events from a failed static-detector path, not as 18 visually confirmed completed placements.
The transferred active detectors did not detect this video reliably: IMG_2628, IMG_3254, and IMG_3262 active detectors were 0/80 sampled frames, and panel_in_transit was 1/80.
The only detector with broad recall was wire_mesh_panel, which fired on every sampled frame and is a known static/resident-material risk screen rather than a countable active-part detector.
Runtime diagnostics were parameter-sensitive: 30s debounce produced 27 non-EOF events, while 60s debounce produced 18 non-EOF events.
The same-timestamp EOF active-track burst was excluded from the blind prediction, but many non-EOF static-fragmentation false positives remain likely.
Several events in the 18-row ledger have tiny travel distances, including 1px, 2px, 3.162px, 4.123px, and 5px, which is inconsistent with robust completed-placement evidence.
next_required_work: Review or build a human timestamp truth ledger for the 4 true events before any event-level validation claim.
Build a real_factory-specific active detector or other legitimate YOLO/event-based runtime path before another promotion-quality app proof attempt.
Do not add real_factory to validation/registry.json unless a future run matches reviewed event truth with no missing or unexpected events.
